# `UDP S->C 0x03/0x26` — Reliable/RemoveWorldItem

**Transport:** UDP  
**Direction:** S->C  
**Identifier:** `0x03/0x26`  
**Status:** verified

## Structure

RemoveWorldItem — server tells the client to remove a specific
entity/object from its local zone view. Verified 2026-05-10
against 37 cross-pcap samples from 17/17 retail captures.

Fixed 5-byte body:

```
[0]      0x26                   sub-opcode (constant)
[1..4]   entity_id LE32          target world-object ID to remove
                                 (typically 0x0001..0x03FF range —
                                 NPC/object IDs)
```

Sample retail bytes:
```
26 42 01 00 00    remove entity 0x0142 (= 322)
26 ed 03 00 00    remove entity 0x03ed (= 1005)
26 bd 03 00 00    remove entity 0x03bd (= 957)
```

Identical wire shape to {@code 0x03/0x27} RequestInfoAboutWorldID
(both are `[sub-op][entity_id LE32]`) — just different semantic
direction (S→C remove vs C→S query).

## Variants

Single 5-byte form across all 253 retail observations. NO size
variation. Some samples (e.g. ODA's `26 00 60 0b 80`) have
unusual high bytes — likely entity IDs in a different namespace
(items, projectiles, vehicles?), but rare (1/37 in samples).

## Observed contexts

Fired when:
- An NPC dies and its corpse despawn timer expires
- A world item is picked up
- A vehicle is dismissed
- An entity walks out of the player's PVS / view-frustum

Top markers correlate with mob-death events (see catalog evidence
for per-capture distribution). Universal — 17/17 captures.

## Open questions

- Single sample with high-bit set (`26 00 60 0b 80` from ODA) —
  entity ID 0x800b6000 = high-namespace ref or different decode?
  Possibly a special non-NPC entity (dropped item, summon, etc.).

## Server-side handler

`server.gameserver.packets.server_udp.RemoveWorldItem` — emits
the verified 5-byte body. Wired in:

- {@link
  server.gameserver.npc.MobDeathHandler} — emitted when an NPC
  dies (task #143).
- Generic despawn paths in `Zone.removeNpc()` and elsewhere.

Tests:
- `RemoveWorldItemByteIdentityTest` (2 tests) — pins the
  sub-opcode + LE32 entity_id encoding.
